{{Quote|It is ancient, a relic that survived the destruction of Malachor. It was always here, far before the Mandalorian Wars. It draws death and hate to it, channels it. Atrocities feed its power, and with its power, it creates hunger. Many Jedi have been consumed by it.|[[Darth Sion]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
The '''Trayus Academy''' was an ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Sith academy|academy]] located on the planet [[Malachor V]]. The [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] discovered the abandoned structure during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. During the [[Jedi Civil War]], he used it to convert Jedi to the Dark Side and train them as [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]]. After the Sith forces were defeated in that conflict, the Academy became the headquarters of the [[Sith Triumvirate]] and continued to serve as the [[Sith training|training]] ground for [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]] until Malachor V was [[Battle of Malachor|destroyed]] in [[3951 BBY]].

==History==
{{Quote|It has been here for thousands of years. It is a place where Sith teachings run strong… It is the threshold of the borders of an ancient empire. Kreia says that it was a place of reflection for the ancient Sith… a gateway to their lands. It drew Lord Revan… and it calls to her as well. She said that the teachings here will lead one to the Sith… the true Sith… and all their shadowed worlds. This place led Revan to the graveyards of Korriban… and beyond.|[[Darth Sion]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
The Trayus Academy was a [[Sith training|training]] center built millennia before the [[Mandalorian Wars]] by a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Empire. Eventually, the Sith abandoned the Academy as well as the planet.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

During the [[Mandalorian Wars]] in the year [[3961 BBY]], a Jedi Knight named [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] rediscovered Malachor while scouting for bases along the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] when he heard stories of a world the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] were forbidden to step foot on according to their culture. Wishing to discover why Malachor V was a taboo world to the Mandalorians, Revan secretly investigated the planet, unaware he was about to discover far more than what he was looking for.<ref name="CotOR" />

Revan came across the Trayus Academy and within he rediscovered the location of the planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] and uncovered evidence that the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] continued to exist beyond the boundaries of known space and continued to pose a threat to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" /> Unbeknownst to even his closest confidants such as [[Darth Malak|Malak]] at the time, Revan constructed a stronghold and used Malachor's dark side energies to bind the most trusted of his followers to his will. The [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassin]] order was founded as a result.<ref name="CotOR">{{LucasArts|url=games/swkotor_sithlords|text=''Chronicles of the Old Republic''|int=Chronicles of the Old Republic}}</ref>

[[File:MalachorV.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The shattered remnants of [[Malachor V]].]]
Returning to the war, Revan led the Republic to victory over the Mandalorians. [[3960 BBY|One year]] after rediscovering the Trayus Academy, Revan forced a final [[Battle of Malachor V|confrontation]] with the Mandalorians above Malachor V. In utter secrecy, he had prepared a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], known as the "[[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]]," which was to be the centerpiece of a trap with which he hoped to bring about a conclusive end to the Mandalorian Wars. Overseeing the device's use was a trusted [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]], [[Meetra Surik]]<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref> (the future Jedi Exile).<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Revan lured the Mandalorians to Malachor, and as the fighting grew intense, on his order, Surik, one of his most trusted generals activated the generators. In the resulting cataclysm, a significant portion of both the Mandalorian and Republic fleets were drawn from orbit into a vast gravity vortex that was powerful enough to crush the countless ships into the planet's crust and fracture the world to its core. Remarkably, the Trayus Academy survived the catastrophic event intact.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Following his victory, Revan [[Mission to Dromund Kaas|traveled]] with [[Darth Malak|Malak]] to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] in search of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]]. There on [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], they encountered the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] and were subdued to the Emperor's will, becoming Darth Revan and Darth Malak.<ref name="Revan" /> The Emperor then ordered them to search for the [[Star Forge]], planning on using it to speed up the building of the Sith armada. During the search,<ref name="T8">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War|int=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War}}</ref> they broke free from the Sith Emperor and founded their own [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]].<ref name="Revan" /> During the [[Jedi Civil War]], circa [[3959 BBY|3959]]–[[3956 BBY]], Darth Revan used the Trayus Academy as one of several secret Sith bases dedicated to the conversion of captured Jedi. Throughout the war, Jedi were captured by his Sith assassins, and other specially trained forces, and brought to the Academy. The corruptive nature of the location weakened their wills so that Revan and his loyal servants could easily lure them to the dark side. In such places, he converted hosts of Jedi into loyal [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]] for his empire.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

A year after the Sith were defeated by the Galactic Republic during the Jedi Civil War, a redeemed Revan departed for the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] in a solitary quest to defeat the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" /><ref name="Revan" /> Meanwhile, [[Kreia]], Revan's former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], suspected that her teachings had led Revan to the dark side and went in search of her pupil—a search that would eventually lead her to Malachor V. Kreia walked upon the surface of the planet and was overcome. She fell to the dark side and became Darth Traya. She studied the planet's secrets and rose to command the Trayus Academy and the Sith Assassins which still lingered there. The Academy was reestablished by Traya to train, convert, and create a new generation of Sith who were used to destroy the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

[[File:TrayusCore.jpg|thumb|right|[[Trayus Core]], the innermost sanctum of the Trayus Academy.]]
Darth Traya soon discovered two [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] in which she saw the potential of the death of the Force. [[Darth Nihilus]] and [[Darth Sion]] joined her cause and together they formed a [[Sith Triumvirate]]. Based within their hidden Academy on Malachor V, the Sith Triumvirate commanded legions of Assassins who [[First Jedi Purge|hunted]] the remaining Jedi to the very brink of extinction. Darth Traya had managed to unite the fragmented Sith forces through their mutual hatred and led them to victory once more, but her success would not save her. In a sudden act of betrayal, her two apprentices joined forces, stripped her of power, and exiled her.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

In [[3951 BBY]], Kreia located Meetra Surik, who was the only Jedi who had fought with Revan against the Mandalorians at Malachor V and yet remained uncorrupted. Wishing to rid the galaxy of her fallen apprentices and ultimately the death of the Force, Kreia orchestrated events to set up a final confrontation between herself and Surik at the Trayus Academy. After [[Battle of Telos IV|slaying Darth Nihilus]], Surik arrived at the Academy to combat the Sith lurking there. She [[Battle of Malachor|invaded the academy]] and eliminated every Sith minion that crossed her path. Moments after defeating Darth Sion and killing Kreia in a series of lightsaber duels, Malachor V was torn apart when the Mass Shadow Generator was reactivated on Surik's orders.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

==Layout==
The entrance of the Trayus Academy from the [[Malachor Depths]] opened onto an antechamber that connected to the eastern and western halls of the complex. The '''Trayus Crescent''', located to the west, was a hall with many alcoves that were frequented by [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)#Variants|Sith commandos]]. To the east, the '''Trayus Proving Grounds''' was a warren of small and cramped rooms in which many [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and Sith commandos could be found. The northern point of both wings led to the Academy's central area, the last bit of open space before the innermost sanctum of the Trayus Academy was known as the [[Trayus Core]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', if the player takes the dark side path, under this non-[[canon]]ical timeline, both Malachor V and the Academy would remain intact.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

It's unknown whether [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|the Sith Empire founded by Adas]] or [[Sith Empire/Legends|the Sith Empire ruled by the Exiles and their descendants]] built the Trayus Academy or when it was abandoned.
